description: Refactor the extension background script's server-only field extraction to prevent silent field drops (the pattern that caused the npmPackageName bug), and fix the CLI's `opentabs plugin search` to post-filter results like the server does.

userStories.title: Extract server-only field helper and spread serverPlugin in handleBgGetFullState
userStories.description: The extension background script extracts server-only fields (source, reviewed, sdkVersion, npmPackageName, update) from raw WebSocket JSON in three places with duplicated field-by-field enumeration. When npmPackageName was added, it was missed in two of three locations, causing a real bug. Refactor to: (1) extract a shared helper for raw JSON extraction used by handleSyncFull and handlePluginUpdate, (2) spread serverPlugin in handleBgGetFullState with explicit overrides so new server-only fields flow through automatically, (3) fix handlePluginUpdate which is also missing npmPackageName.
acceptanceCriteria: A shared extractServerOnlyFields function extracts source, reviewed, npmPackageName, sdkVersion, and update from a Record<string, unknown> with runtime type validation
handleSyncFull cache construction uses the shared helper instead of inline extraction
handlePluginUpdate cache construction uses the shared helper instead of inline extraction

handleBgGetFullState spreads serverPlugin with explicit overrides for tools, tabState, tabs, and permission — new server-only fields added to ConfigStatePlugin flow through without code changes in handleBgGetFullState
npmPackageName flows correctly through all three code paths (handleSyncFull, handlePluginUpdate, handleBgGetFullState)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts (extract helper, update handleSyncFull and handlePluginUpdate)
- platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts (refactor handleBgGetFullState)

Context:
Server-only fields are fields in ConfigStatePlugin (platform/shared/src/index.ts) that do NOT exist in ValidatedPluginPayload or PluginMeta — they come from the MCP server, not from the plugin adapter. Today these are: source, reviewed, npmPackageName, sdkVersion, update.

The bug pattern: when npmPackageName was added to ConfigStatePlugin, it was correctly added to serializePluginForExtension on the server, but the extension's background script dropped it silently in two of three locations because each location manually enumerates which server-only fields to extract.

Current code in message-router.ts, handleSyncFull (line 364-392) — cache construction:
```
const cachePlugins: ConfigStatePlugin[] = uniquePlugins.map(p => {
    const raw = rawPluginMap.get(p.name);
    return {
      name: p.name,
      displayName: p.displayName,
      ...
      source: raw?.source === 'npm' || raw?.source === 'local' ? raw.source : 'local',
      reviewed: raw?.reviewed === true,
      ...(typeof raw?.npmPackageName === 'string' ? { npmPackageName: raw.npmPackageName } : {}),
      ...(typeof raw?.sdkVersion === 'string' ? { sdkVersion: raw.sdkVersion } : {}),
      ...(raw?.update && typeof raw.update === 'object' ? { update: raw.update as { latestVersion: string; updateCommand: string } } : {}),
    };
});
```

Current code in message-router.ts, handlePluginUpdate (line 483-508) — identical pattern, ALSO missing npmPackageName:
```
const updatedPlugin: ConfigStatePlugin = {
    name: validated.name,
    ...
    source: params.source === 'npm' || params.source === 'local' ? params.source : 'local',
    reviewed: params.reviewed === true,
    // BUG: npmPackageName is missing here!
    ...(typeof params.sdkVersion === 'string' ? { sdkVersion: params.sdkVersion } : {}),
    ...(params.update && typeof params.update === 'object' ? { ... } : {}),
};
```

Current code in background-message-handlers.ts, handleBgGetFullState (line 225-238):
```
const { tools: _metaTools, adapterHash: _adapterHash, adapterFile: _adapterFile, ...metaFields } = meta;
return {
  ...metaFields,
  permission: serverPlugin?.permission ?? meta.permission,
  tools,
  tabState,
  tabs,
  source: serverPlugin?.source ?? 'local',
  reviewed: serverPlugin?.reviewed ?? false,
  sdkVersion: serverPlugin?.sdkVersion,
  npmPackageName: serverPlugin?.npmPackageName,
  update: serverPlugin?.update,
  ...(hasLastSeenUrlMap.has(meta.name) && { hasLastSeenUrl: true }),
};
```

Changes:

1. In message-router.ts, add a helper function near the top (after ValidatedPluginPayload):
```typescript
/** Server-only fields extracted from raw WebSocket JSON payloads. */
interface ServerOnlyPluginFields {
  source: 'npm' | 'local';
  reviewed: boolean;
  npmPackageName?: string;
  sdkVersion?: string;
  update?: { latestVersion: string; updateCommand: string };
}

/** Extract server-only fields from a raw JSON payload with runtime type validation. */
const extractServerOnlyFields = (raw: Record<string, unknown> | undefined): ServerOnlyPluginFields => ({
  source: raw?.source === 'npm' || raw?.source === 'local' ? raw.source : 'local',
  reviewed: raw?.reviewed === true,
  ...(typeof raw?.npmPackageName === 'string' ? { npmPackageName: raw.npmPackageName } : {}),
  ...(typeof raw?.sdkVersion === 'string' ? { sdkVersion: raw.sdkVersion } : {}),
  ...(raw?.update && typeof raw.update === 'object'
    ? { update: raw.update as { latestVersion: string; updateCommand: string } }
    : {}),
});
```

2. In handleSyncFull cache construction, replace the inline server-only field extraction with:
```
return {
  name: p.name,
  displayName: p.displayName,
  version: p.version,
  permission: p.permission,
  tabState: 'closed' as const,
  urlPatterns: p.urlPatterns,
  ...(p.excludePatterns.length > 0 ? { excludePatterns: p.excludePatterns } : {}),
  ...(p.homepage ? { homepage: p.homepage } : {}),
  tools: p.tools,
  iconSvg: p.iconSvg,
  iconInactiveSvg: p.iconInactiveSvg,
  iconDarkSvg: p.iconDarkSvg,
  iconDarkInactiveSvg: p.iconDarkInactiveSvg,
  ...extractServerOnlyFields(raw),
};
```

3. In handlePluginUpdate (line 483-508), replace the inline server-only extraction with:
```
const updatedPlugin: ConfigStatePlugin = {
  name: validated.name,
  displayName: validated.displayName,
  version: validated.version,
  permission: validated.permission,
  tabState: newState.state,
  urlPatterns: validated.urlPatterns,
  ...(validated.excludePatterns.length > 0 ? { excludePatterns: validated.excludePatterns } : {}),
  ...(validated.homepage ? { homepage: validated.homepage } : {}),
  tools: validated.tools,
  iconSvg: validated.iconSvg,
  iconInactiveSvg: validated.iconInactiveSvg,
  iconDarkSvg: validated.iconDarkSvg,
  iconDarkInactiveSvg: validated.iconDarkInactiveSvg,
  ...extractServerOnlyFields(params),
};
```
Note: `params` in handlePluginUpdate is already `Record<string, unknown>` so it can be passed directly.

4. In background-message-handlers.ts, handleBgGetFullState — replace the cherry-picked server-only fields with a serverPlugin spread:
```
const serverOnlyDefaults: Pick<ConfigStatePlugin, 'source' | 'reviewed'> = { source: 'local', reviewed: false };
const { tools: _metaTools, adapterHash: _adapterHash, adapterFile: _adapterFile, ...metaFields } = meta;
return {
  ...metaFields,
  ...serverOnlyDefaults,
  ...(serverPlugin ?? {}),
  permission: serverPlugin?.permission ?? meta.permission,
  tools,
  tabState,
  tabs,
  ...(hasLastSeenUrlMap.has(meta.name) && { hasLastSeenUrl: true }),
};
```
This way new server-only fields in ConfigStatePlugin flow through automatically from serverPlugin without manual enumeration.

IMPORTANT: The spread order matters. `metaFields` first (base data), then `serverOnlyDefaults` (defaults for when serverPlugin is undefined), then `serverPlugin` (server cache overrides), then explicit overrides for `permission` (with meta fallback), `tools` (merged), `tabState` (live), `tabs` (live), and `hasLastSeenUrl` (extension-computed). The later spreads overwrite earlier ones.

Note: `serverPlugin.tabState` from the cache is stale ('closed' from init) — the live `tabState` variable (from the tab state cache) must override it. Same for `tabs`. And `serverPlugin.tools` has server permission states but the `tools` variable is the merged version (meta tool definitions + server permission overrides). These three MUST come after the serverPlugin spread to override.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change ValidatedPluginPayload or PluginMeta — these are the extension's internal types, not server types
- Do NOT change how validatePluginPayload works — the security validation boundary must remain
- Do NOT change the ServerStateCache type or how it stores/retrieves data
- The helper must do runtime type checking on all fields (the input is untyped JSON)
- Export extractServerOnlyFields and ServerOnlyPluginFields for test access if needed
- Verify the existing tests still pass — background-message-handlers.test.ts and message-router.test.ts have extensive coverage

userStories.title: Add post-filtering to CLI's opentabs plugin search
userStories.description: The CLI's `opentabs plugin search slack` returns the same 20 results as a query-less search because npm's full-text search returns all opentabs plugins ranked by relevance. The server-side searchNpmPlugins already has post-filtering (added in commit 439ec522), but the CLI has its own independent implementation that lacks it.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs plugin search slack returns only plugins whose short name or description contains 'slack'
opentabs plugin search (no query) still returns all plugins
The short name is derived by stripping the scope and opentabs-plugin- prefix (e.g., @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-slack -> slack)
